purpose: Compact corpus-level intake discipline for new packages, layers, bundles, repos, and corpus-control surfaces before they enter corpus-visible routing.
core_rule: No new package, layer, bundle, repo-level addition, or corpus-control artifact should enter corpus-visible routing until owner, scope, status, reference posture, and public-safe boundary class are classified.
boundary_precheck: Before intake, determine whether candidate material is public-safe, pointer-only, or excluded.

intake_classes.what_it_is: A new package that may carry primary or stable normative meaning.
intake_classes.common_failure_mode: Local framing text starts sounding like mature doctrine before owner, status, and precedence are fixed.

intake_classes.what_it_is: A serious draft package that may later mature but is not yet stable core.
intake_classes.common_failure_mode: Draft wording quietly inflates into stable core by repetition or adjacency.

intake_classes.what_it_is: A package that records observed recurrent practice or protocol shape rather than mature doctrine.
intake_classes.common_failure_mode: Practice note gets misread as universal normative rule.

intake_classes.what_it_is: A package centered on verification, witness, auditability, manifests, or replayable evidence.
intake_classes.common_failure_mode: Verification surface starts acting like ontology or ownership source by itself.

intake_classes.what_it_is: A package that proves buildability, execution constraints, or implementation closure.
intake_classes.common_failure_mode: Implementation convenience silently becomes doctrinal ownership.

intake_classes.what_it_is: A package whose role is recognition, narrative bridge, or human-facing context.
intake_classes.common_failure_mode: Companion illumination starts reading like normative proof or witness evidence.

intake_classes.what_it_is: A new primer, map, policy, lock, registry, or discipline file that organizes corpus reading.
intake_classes.common_failure_mode: A control surface starts creating doctrine instead of routing or classifying it.

intake_classes.intake_class: new boundary note / reserved territory note
intake_classes.what_it_is: A bounded note marking incomplete, protected, or not-yet-settled territory.
intake_classes.common_failure_mode: Incomplete territory is read as completed doctrine because reservation stayed implicit.

intake_classes.what_it_is: An addendum, clause family, extension pack, or adjacent satellite surface.
intake_classes.typical_repo_home: canonical owner repo of the parent package
intake_classes.common_failure_mode: Extension text quietly forks the parent package into parallel canon.

intake_classes.intake_class: new frozen public object / snapshot-backed package
intake_classes.what_it_is: A package whose public identity depends on tag, release, DOI, manifest, or frozen snapshot wording.
intake_classes.typical_repo_home: canonical owner repo with release or DOI surface
intake_classes.common_failure_mode: Moving repo and frozen public object get treated as interchangeable.

fail_closed_rule: If maintainers cannot fix the package class, canonical home, claim impact, or public-safe boundary class, the candidate should remain local or pointer-only until clarified and must not enter corpus-visible routing by implication.
